steps: Submit application via San Jose ePermits portal
Structural plan review by Building Inspection Division (7–14 days)
Permit issued — post on job site
deck.notes: Decks over 30 inches above grade require full structural review. San Jose uses a valuation-based fee table — base $220 plus ~1.2% of project cost.

steps: Submit site plan, floor plan, elevations, and Title 24 energy calc
Plan check (21–35 days; corrections common on first submission)
Inspections: foundation, framing, electrical rough, plumbing rough, insulation, drywall, final
adu.notes: ADUs under 800 sq ft get a 50% plan check fee reduction per California law. San Jose's Pre-Approved ADU Plans Program can cut plan check time significantly.

steps: Submit permit application with scope of work
Plan check (5–10 days for standard kitchen/bath remodels)
remodel.notes: Cosmetic-only work does not require a permit. Any electrical panel upgrade, gas line change, or structural wall removal does.

steps: Submit application at Fresno Development Services or via CityView portal
deck.notes: Fresno fees are among the lower end in California. Decks over 200 sq ft attached to the home require stamped engineering plans.

steps: Submit permit application with site plan and floor plans
Foundation, framing, rough mechanical, and final inspections
adu.notes: Fresno has streamlined ADU review for units under 1,000 sq ft. Impact fees may apply for units over 750 sq ft.

steps: Submit permit application with scope of work
remodel.notes: Most residential remodel permits process within a week in Fresno. Gas or electrical panel upgrades require separate trade permits.

steps: Submit application via SacConnect portal or in person
deck.notes: Decks within 3 feet of a property line require a zoning review. Fire hazard zone properties may face additional review.

steps: Submit site plan, floor plan, and energy calculations
adu.notes: Sacramento offers a pre-approved ADU design library that can bypass plan check entirely for qualifying units — fastest path to permit issuance.

steps: Submit permit application with project description
Over-the-counter review for simple projects (same day)
Plan check for complex work (5–10 days)
remodel.notes: Simple kitchen and bath remodels often qualify for over-the-counter same-day permits in Sacramento. Structural changes require plan check.

steps: Submit application via Oakland's Accela Citizen Access portal
Plan check by Building Services Division (7–14 days)
deck.notes: Hillside properties face additional fire and geotechnical review. Flat-area decks move faster through plan check.

steps: Submit permit application with full plan set
Planning and building plan check (21–45 days)
Inspections: foundation, framing, MEP rough, insulation, final
adu.notes: Oakland has a fast-track program for ADUs under 800 sq ft. Some areas require additional fire sprinkler review.

steps: Plan check (7–14 days; over-the-counter for simple work)
remodel.notes: Minor remodels under $10,000 valuation may qualify for expedited review. Earthquake retrofit work is tracked separately.

steps: Submit application via LADBS ePlanLA portal or at a district office
Plan check (10–21 days; expedited service available for a fee)
deck.notes: Hillside properties in Zone A or B require geotechnical review. LADBS expedited plan check halves review time for an additional fee.

steps: Submit plans via ePlanLA — site plan, floor plan, elevations, Title 24
adu.notes: LA's Standard Plan Program for ADUs bypasses plan check for pre-approved designs. School impact fees apply in most LA areas — budget $3–4/sq ft extra.

steps: Submit permit application via ePlanLA or in person at a district office
Plan check (7–14 days; OTC available for simple scope)
remodel.notes: Structural changes to load-bearing walls require stamped engineering plans. Cosmetic kitchen remodels without MEP changes often qualify for over-the-counter permits.
